mission.title: Install KubeClipper on Linux Nodes
mission.description: KubeClipper is a lightweight web service that provides a friendly web console, APIs, and CLI tools for Kubernetes cluster lifecycle management. It uses the kcctl CLI to deploy components via SSH to your nodes. KubeClipper supports deploying and managing multiple Kubernetes clusters across bare-metal and VM environments.

steps.title: Install kcctl CLI
steps.description:
Download and install the kcctl command-line tool on your management machine:
```bash
curl -sfL https://oss.kubeclipper.io/get-kubeclipper.sh | bash -
```
Verify the installation:
```bash
kcctl version
```

steps.title: Deploy KubeClipper (All-in-One)
steps.description:
For a quick single-node deployment, use the AIO (All-in-One) mode. This installs etcd, kc-server, and kc-agent on the same node:
```bash
kcctl deploy
```
This command will prompt for your node's SSH credentials and deploy all KubeClipper components. By default, the web console will be available on port 80.

For a multi-node deployment, specify server and agent nodes explicitly:
```bash
kcctl deploy --server <server-ip> --agent <agent-ip1>,<agent-ip2> --pk-file ~/.ssh/id_rsa
```

steps.title: Access the web console
steps.description:
After deployment completes, access the KubeClipper web console in your browser:
```
http://<server-ip>
```
Default credentials:
- Username: `admin`
- Password: `Thinkbig1`

Change the default password after your first login.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that all KubeClipper components are running:
```bash
kcctl get node
kcctl get component
```
You should see your server and agent nodes listed with a 'Running' status.

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show all KubeClipper components running via `kcctl get component`. The web console will be accessible at http://<server-ip> with the default admin credentials. You can then use the console or kcctl CLI to create and manage Kubernetes clusters.
codeSnippets:
kcctl get node
kcctl get component

uninstall.title: Clean up KubeClipper
uninstall.description:
Remove all KubeClipper components from your nodes using kcctl:
```bash
kcctl clean --all
```
This removes etcd, kc-server, kc-agent, and all associated data from the deployed nodes.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeClipper
upgrade.description:
Download the latest kcctl CLI and run the upgrade command:
```bash
curl -sfL https://oss.kubeclipper.io/get-kubeclipper.sh | bash -
kcctl upgrade
```

troubleshooting.title: SSH connection failed during deploy
troubleshooting.description:
If kcctl deploy fails with SSH errors, ensure:
1. SSH access is configured (key-based or password) between the management machine and target nodes
2. The target user has sudo privileges
3. The SSH port (default 22) is open
```bash
ssh <user>@<node-ip> 'sudo whoami'
```

troubleshooting.title: Web console not accessible
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the web console:
1. Check that kc-server is running: `kcctl get component`
2. Verify port 80 is not blocked by a firewall
3. Check kc-server logs for errors

troubleshooting.title: Node not joining
troubleshooting.description:
If an agent node is not appearing in `kcctl get node`:
1. Verify the agent is running on the target node
2. Check network connectivity between agent and server nodes
3. Ensure the node meets minimum requirements: 2 CPU cores, 2GB RAM

prerequisites.description: KubeClipper deploys to Linux nodes (not into an existing Kubernetes cluster). You need: Linux nodes with SSH access, sudo privileges, 2+ CPU cores, 2+ GB RAM per node, and curl installed on the management machine.
